claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 14 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 14 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Blake
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 14 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for UK Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin the Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child or children you're claiming for.**

**How old is your child (or children)?**

Please let me know:
- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 years old or over**

If you have multiple children of different ages, please tell me their ages and I'll assess each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh hi, thanks for helping me with this. I've got two children - Alex who's 8 and Blake who's 14. So they're both under 16.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thanks for letting me know about Alex (8) and Blake (14). Since both children are under 16, they automatically meet the age requirement for Child Benefit.

Let me check the next requirement for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about work status:

**Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work at all)?**

Given their ages (8 and 14), I expect they're not working full-time, but I just need to confirm this to proceed.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them work at all - they're just in school. Alex is only 8, so definitely not working, and Blake's 14 and just focuses on school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, that makes sense! Since neither Alex nor Blake are working, they both meet this requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are both Alex and Blake NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right?**

This refers to benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that a child might claim for themselves. Given their ages and that they're in school, this is unlikely, but I just need to confirm neither child is receiving these types of benefits.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them get any benefits in their own name. They're just kids in school, so nothing like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that's what I expected. Let me move on to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake currently doing any apprenticeships?**

Since they're 8 and 14 and in school, I'd assume they're not in any apprenticeship programmes, but I just need to confirm this. 

(For reference: most apprenticeships would disqualify a child from Child Benefit, except Foundation Apprenticeships in Wales which are approved.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they're not doing apprenticeships - they're too young for that anyway. They're just in regular school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Exactly right! Since neither Alex nor Blake are in any apprenticeships, they both pass this check too. Let me continue to the next requirement.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about your residency status.

**Do you meet UK residency requirements?**

You can qualify through ANY of the following:

1. **Normally live in the UK** - this is the most common route
2. **Have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **Have pre-settled status** and are working/earning above the National Insurance threshold
4. **Have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **Have pre-settled status** with sufficient resources to support yourself and family
6. **Have pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient resources
7. **Have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national
8. **Crown servant** living abroad or in certain qualifying countries

Do any of these apply to you? For most people, the first option (normally living in the UK) is the relevant one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester. So the first one applies to me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, living in Manchester means you meet the UK residency requirement. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your responsibility for Alex and Blake.

**Are you responsible for both children?**

You can qualify through ANY of the following:

1. **Living with the children** - they live with you in your home
2. **Contributing to their upkeep** - you pay at least the Child Benefit amount (or equivalent) towards their care (food, clothes, pocket money, housing, etc.)
3. **Paying maintenance** - you make maintenance payments under a court order or agreement

Which of these applies to your situation with Alex and Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They both live at home with me, so yes, the first one - they live with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, since Alex and Blake both live with you, you meet the responsibility requirement. Let me check the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex or Blake?**

For example:
- Another parent or guardian
- A former partner
- Another family member

If someone else is claiming, have you agreed between yourselves who should receive the benefit, or is there a dispute?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for them. It's just me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, that keeps things straightforward. Let me continue to the next check.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check about where Alex and Blake are currently living.

**What is the children's current living situation?**

- **Normal living** - They live at home with you in a regular family setting
- **In hospital** - Currently staying in hospital
- **In care** - Currently in local authority care
- **Fostered** - Currently in a fostering arrangement

Which applies to Alex and Blake?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: They're in normal living - just at home with me in a regular family setting.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
We're almost there! Just one final check:

**Is the local council NOT paying towards Alex and Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

In other words, are you supporting them without financial contributions from the local council for their housing or upkeep?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: That's right - the council isn't paying anything towards them. I'm supporting them myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent news! That was the final check. Let me confirm the outcome for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
---

## 🎉 Great News! You ARE Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on our assessment, you are **eligible for Child Benefit** for both **Alex** and **Blake**.

### Summary:

| Child | Age | Eligible? |
|-------|-----|-----------|
| Alex | 8 | ✅ Yes |
| Blake | 14 | ✅ Yes |

### Why You Qualify:
- ✅ Both children are under 16
- ✅ Neither child is working
- ✅ Neither child receives benefits in their own right
- ✅ Neither child is in a disqualifying apprenticeship
- ✅ You live in the UK (Manchester)
- ✅ Both children live with you
- ✅ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them
- ✅ The local council is not paying towards their care

### Next Steps to Claim:

1. **Apply online or by post** using form CH2
2. **Provide proof of identity** for yourself and both children
3. **Provide birth certificates** for Alex and Blake
4. **Be aware**: If you or your partner earns over £60,000, you may be subject to the High Income Child Benefit Charge
5. **Payments** are usually made every 4 weeks (or weekly if you're a single parent or receiving certain benefits)

📎 **More information and how to claim**: https://www.gov.uk/child-benefit/how-to-claim

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex (age 8) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old (automatic age qualification), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant who is responsible for them, no other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, living in a normal family setting, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 14) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old (automatic age qualification), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant who is responsible for them, no other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, living in a normal family setting, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements. Both children (Alex, 8, and Blake, 14) are under 16, not working, not receiving their own benefits, not in apprenticeships, and live at home with the claimant in a normal family setting. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility criteria are satisfied for both children.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.question: Are you responsible for the child?
response.help_text: Responsibility means living with child OR contributing to their upkeep

criteria: You pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or equivalent in kind) towards their care
Contributions include: food, clothes, pocket money, housing, presents
Can be weekly, monthly, or lump sum payments

paths.description: Paying maintenance under court order or agreement
criteria: Payments made towards child's maintenance under court order or agreement
Amount equals or exceeds Child Benefit payment

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to child location checks

response.description: Check if another person is claiming Child Benefit for this child
response.routing_logic: If no other claimant, proceed. If other claimant exists and parties agree who should claim, route accordingly. If dispute exists, defer to HMRC for arbitration.

response.description: Determine if special rules apply based on child's location
response.routing_logic: Check if child is in hospital, care, fostered, or living normally

response.question: Is the local council NOT paying towards the child's accommodation or maintenance?
response.help_text: You generally cannot get Child Benefit if the local council is paying for the child

response.description: Applicant meets all criteria for Child Benefit
response.reason: Meets all Child Benefit eligibility requirements
next_steps: Apply online or by post using form CH2
Provide proof of identity for yourself and the child
Provide child's birth certificate or adoption certificate
Be aware of High Income Child Benefit Charge if you or partner earns over £60,000
Payments made every 4 weeks (or weekly if single parent or receiving certain benefits)

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex (age 8) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old (automatic age qualification), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant who is responsible for them, no other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, living in a normal family setting, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

Blake (age 14) meets all eligibility criteria: under 16 years old (automatic age qualification), not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, not in an apprenticeship, lives with the claimant who is responsible for them, no other person is claiming Child Benefit for them, living in a normal family setting, and the local council is not paying towards their accommodation or maintenance.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester), meeting residency requirements. Both children (Alex, 8, and Blake, 14) are under 16, not working, not receiving their own benefits, not in apprenticeships, and live at home with the claimant in a normal family setting. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for them, and the local council is not contributing to their care. All eligibility criteria are satisfied for both children.
